records.display_name: Park Avenue Viaduct Repair

description: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates. This state-of-good-repair effort will ensure the safety of the public and the railroad, improve commuter rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels by using modern materials. Major construction expected to begin in fall of 2023, repaired viaduct anticipated to be in service in 2027.

Replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023, anticipated in service 2027.

NYC legal agreements executed in July 2022. Inspection and flag reports completed and submitted. Design-Build Construction Contract awarded in December 2022. The Park Avenue Viaduct is the main artery of the Metro-North system and carries all trains into and out of Grand Central Terminal.

A state-of-good-repair effort to replace this critical bridge carrying Metro-North service into Grand Central Terminal. The project will ensure the safety of the public and railroad, improve rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels.

Critical state-of-good repair effort to replace the over 100-year-old Park Avenue Viaduct in Manhattan. Pre-construction work includes surveying, soil boring, rock core sampling, and geoprobes. Substructure construction (new foundations and columns) starting later this summer. Will include an MTA Arts & Design art installation over E 116 Street.
Acquisition of permanent and temporary property interests in support of replacement of the Park Avenue viaduct

Phase 2 of the Park Avenue Viaduct replacement on Metro-North Railroad, from East 127th Street to midblock between East 131st and East 132nd Streets. Performed under contract MN-171020 with Halmar International, LLC.
Large-scale replacement project for the Park Avenue Viaduct which dates back to the late 1800s. Phase 1 spans from 115 to 123 Streets.

MTA Construction & Development is currently in the midst of a large-scale replacement project for the Park Avenue Viaduct. Phase 1 spans from 115th to 123rd Streets on Park Avenue. Progress includes replacing 2,200 feet of steel third rail with new aluminum third rail, relocating signal power feeder and communication cables. One of two gantry systems has been erected. The structure dates back to the late 1800s and carries approximately 750 trains and thousands of customers to and from Grand Central Terminal every weekday.

Modification to add Phase 2 of the Park Avenue Viaduct replacement, from East 127th Street to midblock between East 131st and East 132nd Streets, to the existing Phase 1 design-build contract with Halmar International

Replace over 130-year-old railroad viaduct from E115th Street to E123rd Street (Phase 1) and E127th Street to E132nd Street (Phase 2), along Park Avenue in Harlem including foundations, structure, tracks, power, communications and signals
Reconstruction of the Park Avenue Viaduct.
Continuation of Park Avenue Viaduct Span renewal requiring extensive track outages on all three lines
Ongoing state of good repair project for Metro-North Railroad
Design-build and best value determination
Phase One and Phase Two proceeding simultaneously; 780 feet of track installed, more than half of bridge replacements completed, yielding cost savings.
Continuation of the Park Avenue Viaduct replacement projects

Phase 1 replaced 8,240 track feet of the aging 132-year-old structure through 128 bridge installations, all without disrupting Metro-North service. The project is $93 million under budget and 51 months ahead of schedule.

Replacement of over 130-year-old railroad viaduct from E115th Street to E123rd Street (Phase 1) and E127th Street to E132nd Street (Phase 2), along Park Avenue in Harlem, including foundations, structure, tracks, power, communications and signals.

The Park Avenue Viaduct is over 100 years old and carries every train serving Grand Central Terminal, which is 98% of all Metro-North mainline service. Phase One was completed ahead of schedule and under budget.

Replacement of Park Avenue Viaduct. Phase One completed October 2025 with full superstructure renewal. Phase Two substructure work for Track 4 completed June 2025. Achieved $93 million in savings, delivered 51 months ahead of schedule without a single train delay. IEC reports 70% complete, on budget and schedule.
Phase 1 bridge replacement work completed 51 months ahead of schedule and $93 million under budget, with no impact to Metro-North's operations

Phase 1 replaced the viaduct from East 115th to East 123rd Street and included building new foundations, piers, superstructure, third rail, continuously welded rail, and signal power and communication cables. Nine weekend outages between July and November 2025 allowed crews to replace 34 bridges.
Phase 2 will extend the replacement to 17 additional spans from East 127th to East 132nd Street. Awarded in May 2024, scheduled for completion in late 2027.
Continuation of the Park Avenue Viaduct project, an ongoing infrastructure project accommodated by schedule changes

Retrofits parts of the 129-year-old Park Avenue Viaduct carrying 98% of MNR trains. Installation of retrofit brackets on fascia columns between E 115 St and E 123 St, and between E 128 St and E 132 St. Budget $10.3M. Precursor to a more extensive replacement project.
Replacement of sections of the Park Avenue Viaduct in phases. Phase 1 construction scheduled to commence in 2023.

Replacement of sections of the Park Avenue Viaduct in phases between 115th Street to 123rd Street (Phase 1) and 127th Street to 132nd Street (Phase 2) and replacement of certain structural elements between East 123rd Street and East 128th St (Phase 3)
First phase accelerated; additional scope for segments previously planned for 2025-2029 program
Accelerated replacement of Metro-North's 125+ year-old Park Avenue Viaduct with 7 blocks now slated for reconstruction

Replace or rehabilitate major segments of the aging Park Avenue Viaduct, an elevated steel structure carrying four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110th Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge. Originally constructed in 1893.

Partial replacement and repair of Metro-North Railroad's Park Avenue Viaduct. RFP issued. Design-build contract award on track for later this year. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023. Repaired viaduct anticipated in service in 2027. Work between East 115 Street and East 132 Street.
status: Phase One completed ahead of schedule and under budget

payload.description: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates. This state-of-good-repair effort will ensure the safety of the public and the railroad, improve commuter rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels by using modern materials. Major construction expected to begin in fall of 2023, repaired viaduct anticipated to be in service in 2027.
payload.location: Park Avenue, Harlem, E115th St to E123rd St (Phase 1) and E127th St to E132nd St (Phase 2)

location_normalized.raw_text: Park Avenue, Harlem, E115th St to E123rd St (Phase 1) and E127th St to E132nd St (Phase 2)

records.raw_text: The program was created as part of the Park Avenue Viaduct Project, which will replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge.

records.display_name: LIRR on behalf of itself and Metro-North seeks Board approval for a public works contract to Sperry Rail, Inc. for Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection services. The contract has a term of 5 years.

payload.description: LIRR on behalf of itself and Metro-North seeks Board approval for a public works contract to Sperry Rail, Inc. for Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection services. Term of 5 years. Contract price approximately $41.3 million for both railroads for the full five-year term. Will provide FRA mandated inspection utilizing latest technologies in ultrasonic testing, joint bar detection, and eddy current testing.
payload.project_name: Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection services contract with Sperry Rail, Inc.

records.raw_text: LIRR on behalf of itself and Metro-North seeks Board approval for a public works contract to Sperry Rail, Inc. for Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection services. The contract has a term of 5 years.

payload.project_name: Harlem Line signal box elevation at MP 25.7

records.raw_text: elevation of the Harlem Line signal box at MP 25.7 located at the grade crossing at Cleveland Street and Lakeview Avenue. This elevation project addresses recurring flooding issues

records.display_name: Climate Resilience Roadmap

description: Assessment of current and future climate vulnerabilities, 10 climate resilience strategies, and implementation framework including capital projects, design practices, operating actions, and interagency actions

Comprehensive strategy necessitating a $6 billion investment over the next decade to fortify MTA infrastructure against challenges posed by climate change. From upgrading train yards to enhancing signal systems.

MTA has embarked on implementing a new Climate Resilience Roadmap. Comprehensive strategy will necessitate a $6 billion investment over the next decade to fortify Metro-North's infrastructure against climate change.

payload.description: Assessment of current and future climate vulnerabilities, 10 climate resilience strategies, and implementation framework including capital projects, design practices, operating actions, and interagency actions

records.display_name: Park Avenue Viaduct Repair part_of_program 2020-2024 Capital Program
records.raw_text: The program was created as part of the Park Avenue Viaduct Project, which will replace the elevated steel structure that carries four tracks along Park Avenue between East 110 Street and the Harlem River Lift Bridge.
